summary: Small tooling fix for macOS builds: the project tool no longer selects `wibo` on Darwin. The PR body shows macOS attempted to run `build/tools/wibo` instead of `wine`, causing `/bin/sh: build/tools/wibo: cannot execute binary file`; the diff restricts `use_wibo()` to Linux only. The PR was closed without merge according to the provided context, and there were no review comments.

key_files.role: Changed platform gating in `use_wibo()` so the compiler wrapper uses wibo only on Linux, not macOS.

what_changed: `tools/project.py` changed one condition in `use_wibo()` from allowing `sys.platform == "linux" or sys.platform == "darwin"` to allowing only `sys.platform == "linux"`.
The PR targeted a macOS build failure where the build tried to execute `build/tools/wibo` for the Metrowerks compiler wrapper path.
The failure shown in the PR body happened during a Ninja compile command for `src/melee/pl/plstale.c`, ending with `cannot execute binary file`.
After the change, the PR body reports the build reaching the `PROGRESS` step successfully.
smart_moves: Fixed the platform-selection predicate at the source of the wrapper choice instead of adding a local workaround to a single build command.
Kept the change minimal: one-line conditional update, reducing risk to unrelated compiler wrapper behavior.
Preserved the existing architecture checks and `self.wrapper is None` guard while narrowing only the unsupported OS case.
decomp_lessons: Build-wrapper selection should be gated by both architecture and operating system; a binary runnable on Linux architectures is not necessarily runnable on macOS.
For cross-platform decomp tooling, avoid assuming Darwin can use the same prebuilt helper binaries as Linux even when both report `x86_64` or `arm64`.
A matching/decomp progress command can be used as a practical smoke test after build-tooling changes, but this PR provides only body evidence, not CI or review confirmation.
naming_conventions: `use_wibo()` is the boolean predicate controlling whether the build should use wibo.
`wibo_tag`, `wrapper`, and `compiler_wrapper` appear to be existing project-tooling names for selecting compiler execution wrappers.
No new symbols or naming conventions were introduced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or function-matching tactics were involved; this was a Python build-tooling platform gate.
The failure occurred while compiling decomp source with `mwcceppc.exe` through `sjiswrap.exe`, showing that wrapper correctness is a prerequisite for normal matching builds.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The PR was closed with `merged_at: null`, so the slice does not prove this exact patch landed.
follow_up_queries: Check whether a later PR or commit applied the same `use_wibo()` Darwin exclusion after this PR was closed unmerged.
Search build-tooling history for macOS wrapper selection between `wibo`, `wine`, and any user-provided `wrapper` configuration.
Verify whether documentation tells macOS users how to install/configure `wine` for the Metrowerks compiler tools.
